key_insight: A locking-free mixed finite element eigenvalue formulation for elasticity can be built using only pseudostress, avoiding any symmetry enforcement while still recovering displacement by postprocessing.
engineering_relevance: This enables more robust and accurate solid mechanics eigenanalysis for nearly incompressible or locking-prone problems in 2D and 3D.
method: Mixed FEM in a pure pseudostress tensor formulation
Tensorial standard finite element families for H(div) discretization
A priori and a posteriori analysis using non-compact operator theory
evidence_signal: The abstract reports convergence, a priori error estimates, and a posteriori reliability/efficiency, validated on multiple geometric and physical test cases.
watch_item: Because displacement is recovered through postprocessing, implementation must carefully preserve the mixed-space stability and estimator reliability across meshes and material regimes.
paper_title: A locking free mixed FEM based on a pure pseudostress based formulation for the elasticity eigenproblem
